title: STOP MADURO Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.
plain_summary: The STOP MADURO Act lets the US government offer up to 100 million dollars as a reward for information that helps catch and convict Nicolas Maduro. Maduro is the leader of Venezuela who has been charged with serious crimes like drug trafficking and terrorism. The reward money would only come from selling things the government has already taken away from Maduro and his officials because of sanctions. This means the US would not use taxpayer money for the reward.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Securing Timely Opportunities for Payment and Maximizing Awards for Detaining Unlawful Regime Officials Act of 2025 or the STOP MADURO Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found these facts. In 2020, the Trump administration filed criminal charges against Nicolas Maduro Moros, who leads the government of Venezuela, and other officials who work for him. They were charged with being part of a drug trafficking conspiracy that helps terrorists. This crime has a minimum sentence of twenty years in prison and could result in life in prison. They were also charged with planning to bring cocaine into the United States. This crime has a minimum sentence of ten years in prison and could result in life in prison. They were charged with using and carrying machine guns and explosive weapons while committing these drug crimes. This crime has a minimum sentence of thirty years in prison and could result in life in prison. They were also charged with planning to use machine guns and explosive weapons while committing these drug crimes. This crime could result in life in prison. On March 26, 2020, a federal prosecutor named Geoffrey S. Berman said that Nicolas Maduro ran a drug trafficking partnership with a terrorist group called FARC for twenty years. He said that Maduro and others corrupted the government of Venezuela to protect these crimes. He said that Maduro deliberately wanted to flood the United States with cocaine to harm our country and used cocaine as a weapon. Another federal prosecutor named Ariana Fajardo Orshan said that in recent years, federal law enforcement has brought criminal charges against many high level officials and their partners, which resulted in seizing about four hundred fifty million dollars.

sections.title: Section 3 Increasing the Maximum Reward Amount for Information Leading to the Arrest and Conviction of Nicolas Maduro Moros Through the Department of State Rewards Program
sections.text: The Secretary of State can pay up to one hundred million dollars as a reward to one or more people who provide information that directly leads to the arrest and conviction of Nicolas Maduro Moros. This reward amount is allowed even though current law sets lower limits for rewards. The money for this reward must come only from selling assets that the government is holding back from Nicolas Maduro Moros, officials who work for the Maduro government, and their partners in crime. These assets are being held by the President or by a Treasury Department office called the Office of Foreign Assets Control under various laws and executive orders. These include the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act, the Venezuela Defense of Human Rights and Civil Society Act of 2014, Executive Order 13692 which blocks property and stops entry of certain people contributing to problems in Venezuela, Executive Order 13850 which blocks property of additional people contributing to problems in Venezuela, Executive Order 13884 which blocks property of the Government of Venezuela, or any other sanctions law.
